raw_consumer_statement: In XX/XX/year>, my wife and I departed on a cruise from XXXX, XXXX. We were only there for about XXXX hours and walked around town. The only place I used my Barclays cc that day was at the XXXX XXXX. We then departed on cruise. When I retirned hime, I noticed there was a XXXX charge on my card on the same day as the XXXX XXXX. I did not make this charge so I immediately reported the fraud. Barclays issued me a new card. the XXXX charge was removed and I continued using the new card and paying my bills. I usually pay the bill in full each month and was not paying attention to statements. A couple months later I notice the XXXX is back on my statement. I contacted Barclays and they are saying that since it was chip inserted, I had to have made the charge. The card did not leave my possession except for the time I paid the bill at the restaurant. I have been complaining about this to no avail. My only fault was not immediately seeing the charge back on my bill although I dont think it would have made a difference since they insist I made the charge. Even their own rep over the phone told me they thought the company was a fraudulent company although they cant even tell me who it was! I have an XXXX credit rating. I did not achieve this by not paying my bills. My only mistake was not seeing the charge back on my bill as I received no written correspondence in the mail from them regarding the fraud complaint. I had written to the President of Barclays USA and they had someone call but he was not of much help and never returned phone calls. I had also written to Barclays in XXXX as the parent company. Apparently they do not even bother to answer customers international letters.
